what_it_does: Spawns the built sync-cli.js as a subprocess with the environment guard EPISODIC_MEMORY_SUMMARIZER_GUARD=1 and asserts that it exits 0 silently, writes a guard message to stderr, and does not emit normal sync output to stdout; it also verifies the guard short-circuits execution when --background is passed.
observations: Uses vitest describe/it/expect and child_process spawnSync.
Resolves SYNC_CLI from the test file location up to repo root under dist/sync-cli.js.
Sets a 5000 ms timeout and utf-8 encoding for spawned processes.
First test checks exit status 0, stderr matching /skipping sync.*subprocess/i, and stdout not matching /Syncing conversations/.
Second test checks exit status 0 and stdout not matching /Sync started in background/ when --background is used with the guard.
standouts: Tests a reentrancy/circuit-breaker guard implemented via an environment variable.
Covers both default foreground invocation and the --background detached-child path.
Assertions focus on absence of normal output as much as presence of guard behavior.
concerns: Hardcodes dist/sync-cli.js, so the test depends on a prior build step.
Only exercises the guard-when-set case, not the guard-absent behavior.
5000 ms timeout may be tight on slow CI runners.
notes: An integration test that verifies the sync CLI bails out early when an episodic-memory summarizer guard environment variable is set, including before a background fork.
questions: What is the exact normal stdout/stderr output of sync-cli when the guard is not set?
How is the --background detached child implemented in sync-cli.js?
Is the dist directory produced by the same package's build, or must it be present before testing?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to test sync-cli reentrancy guard behavior, and the file does exactly that for both plain and --background invocations.
intention_signals: The file appears to be trying to ensure that the sync CLI can detect when it is being invoked recursively by a summarizer subprocess and abort before doing real work or forking a background process.
novelty: The guard is named EPISODIC_MEMORY_SUMMARIZER_GUARD, tying a generic reentrancy guard to a specific episodic-memory summarizer concern.
contribution: Provides integration-level coverage that the sync CLI's guard env variable causes an early, silent, successful exit and suppresses both normal sync output and background-fork output.
summary_for_embedding: A vitest integration test that spawns dist/sync-cli.js with EPISODIC_MEMORY_SUMMARIZER_GUARD=1 to verify the CLI exits silently before syncing or forking a background process.
intention_for_embedding: To guard against recursive invocation of the sync CLI by an episodic memory summarizer subprocess, including when --background is requested.
